The target benchmark of 7.0 on IMDb is extremely high for live-action or hybrid video game adaptations. Highly successful and well-received movies in this genre, such as 'Sonic the Hedgehog' (1 & 2) and 'Detective Pikachu', have plateaued at exactly 6.5 on IMDb. Even 'The Super Mario Bros. Movie', which received widespread fan acclaim and massive box office success, only managed to hit exactly 7.0. For 'A Minecraft Movie' to achieve a 7.0, it would need to match Mario's universal appeal. However, early indicators heavily point the other way: the first trailer suffered massive backlash, the live-action format has been heavily criticized by the games' older fanbase, and test-screenings report the movie has "Kids Choice Awards energy" aimed strictly at 10-year-olds. The director has admitted his focus was solely on satisfying 10-year-olds. Because IMDb users skew significantly older, a children's movie lacking broad crossover appeal is almost certain to receive lower scores from adult viewers, as they typically do. Considering the ceiling set by better-received peers and the strong negative audience sentiment thus far, the probability of hitting a 7.0 is very low.

The market price was 0.76 on March 6. Trump's massive 25% tariffs on Canada/Mexico and +10% on China took effect on March 4. While on March 6 he temporarily exempted USMCA-compliant goods from Canada/Mexico until April 2, this only covers about 38% of Canadian and 50% of Mexican imports. The remaining goods from these top trade partners are still subject to 25% duties. Combined with the baseline tariff of ~2.4%, the extra 10% on China, and a 25% global steel/aluminum tariff starting March 12, the underlying math suggests the US weighted average tariff is already on track to exceed 6% in Q2 2025 unless all tariffs are rapidly reversed. The high probability (0.75) reflects the fact that only one quarter needs to average >= 6% to resolve YES, and Trump's active use of aggressive duties already places the effective rate near or above that threshold.

The question asks if South Korea's TFR will be higher in 2025 than its 2023 baseline of 0.72. Preliminary data from Statistics Korea released in late February 2025 shows that the 2024 TFR already increased to 0.75, reversing a nearly decade-long decline. More importantly, South Korea experienced a massive 14.9% surge in marriages in 2024—the largest spike since 1970. Because out-of-wedlock births are exceedingly rare in South Korea, marriage is a highly reliable leading indicator for births over the subsequent 1–2 years. Official government projections (from the Presidential Committee on Ageing Society) forecast that the TFR will further grow to 0.79 in 2025, with total births expected to exceed 250,000. While the short-lived martial law attempt in December 2024 could inject political and economic uncertainty that depresses conceptions, this would predominantly impact births in late 2025 (October–December). The births for the first 8–9 months of 2025 are already largely fixed by conceptions occurring in 2024 during the marriage boom. Consequently, 2025 has immense structural momentum to comfortably remain above the 0.72 mark. Given the 14.9% marriage boom and the 2024 preliminary actuals of 0.75, the probability of beating 0.72 in 2025 is very high.

The consensus tracking from NRG and BoxOfficePro puts 'A Minecraft Movie' roughly around a $55M - $75M domestic opening weekend (pegged specifically at $58M by NRG). Movies opening in this range outside of the holiday corridor almost never gross $1 billion worldwide, usually finishing in the $350M–$500M tier globally (similar to the Sonic the Hedgehog movies and Detective Pikachu). The few films to cross $1B off such an opening, like Zootopia or Aquaman, had distinct advantages like holiday legs or near-perfect reception. Conversely, 'A Minecraft Movie' has faced starkly negative reactions to its trailers, creating significant headwinds for strong word-of-mouth or 'legs'. With presales currently pacing behind Sonic 3 and FNAF — both of which maxed out well below $400M worldwide — there is no empirical pathway for the film to reach $1 billion globally without an unprecedented, all-time historic tracking error coupled with staggering international reception. The previous Manifold market value of 0.20 on March 6 likely reflects early optimism based purely on the huge IP footprint before this concrete tracking data shattered that thesis on March 13. My final estimate is 3%.

Musk has targeted June 2025 for a public autonomous ride-hailing service in Austin, TX. Because Texas lacks strict autonomous vehicle regulations, Tesla could legally launch a very restricted, geofenced, teleoperations-assisted service by the end of 2025 to meet these claims. However, Tesla's historical record on autonomous deadlines is notoriously poor, often missing by multiple years. The recent news in late February 2025 that Tesla has applied for a human-driven ride-hailing permit in California suggests that true, unsupervised driverless capability across multiple domains may not be as close as Musk claims. While a limited Texas launch keeps the probability alive, the lack of current driverless testing in Austin combined with Tesla's history makes a successful 2025 launch less likely than the market's 48% consensus. I estimate it at 28%.

DeepMind achieved silver (4 out of 6 problems) at IMO 2024, missing the gold threshold by just 1 point. Since then, the frontier of AI reasoning has advanced rapidly, with models like OpenAI's o3 achieving gold at the IOI (Informatics Olympiad) and DeepSeek R1 demonstrating massive jumps in math capabilities. DeepMind's AlphaProof/AlphaGeometry2 systems will have an additional year of refinement for IMO 2025 (scheduled for July 2025). The resolution relies on Eliezer Yudkowsky or Paul Christiano acknowledging the success. While Eliezer previously complained about the formal Grand Challenge requiring models to be open-sourced, he emphasized his bet is on the "technical capability existing" by the end of 2025, suggesting neither judge will likely deny a YES resolution due to a pedantic open-source technicality if the AI is strictly tested under IMO time limits (as DeepMind did in 2024). Given this trajectory, it is highly likely that an AI will clear the gold threshold (typically ~29 points or 5/6 problems) at IMO 2025. A 0.84 probability accurately reflects this strong technical expectation while leaving room for the small chance that AI surprisingly struggles on the 2025 problem set (e.g., combinatorics bottlenecks) or a judging technicality unexpectedly derails resolution.

To forecast whether OpenAI will hint at or claim to have AGI by the end of 2025, we have several strong opposing forces. 

Pushing the probability DOWN:
1. In December 2024, it was reported that OpenAI and Microsoft have an internal agreement defining an AGI system as one generating $100 billion in profit. Hitting this metric by 2025 end is practically impossible given OpenAI's current revenues.
2. OpenAI is actively attempting to remove the 'AGI clause' (which excludes Microsoft from AGI IP licenses) to secure future funding. If this clause is deleted, the specific 'hint' mentioned in the resolution criteria (excluding a system from MS IP licenses) becomes impossible.
3. Completely severing ties with Microsoft would be financial suicide for OpenAI, creating a massive disincentive for the board to officially declare AGI under the current structure.

Pushing the probability UP:
1. Sam Altman has aggressively shifted the narrative in January 2025. He stated OpenAI now 'knows how to build AGI' and that agents will enter the workforce in 2025. 
2. Altman is purposefully downplaying 'AGI' ('it will matter much less') while pivoting to hyping 'Artificial Superintelligence' (ASI). This sets the stage for OpenAI to make a 'weak claim' to AGI for an upcoming 2025 model (like GPT-5) without inciting panic or claiming it has reached the terminal endpoint of AI.
3. The resolution criteria are somewhat subjective and allow for a 'weak claim to AGI by OpenAI's official actions or statements' to count. 

Given that the Manifold market was at 0.19 on March 6, 2025, the market has already digested these January statements and December leaks. Altman's rhetoric heavily lays the groundwork for a weak claim in 2025, but the restrictive financial metrics ($100B) and contract negotiations act as a bottleneck. Balancing these factors, I am slightly more bullish than the March 6 baseline due to the subjectivity of 'weak claim' and Altman's clear intent to water down the AGI milestone.

The market estimate of 51% likely overreacted to the sharp drop in the Atlanta Fed's Q1 GDPNow forecast (which hit -2.8% in early March). The negative print is driven primarily by a massive jump in imports as businesses front-run Trump tariffs. While this could result in a negative Q1 advance estimate, national accounting mathematics mean this tariff anxiety will act as a baseline effect that strongly pushes Q2 GDP positive (as imports revert to normal levels or fall). Because the resolution defines a recession strictly as two consecutive quarters of negative GDP, and the resolution date is Dec 26, 2025 (meaning only Q1+Q2 or Q2+Q3 are viable windows, since Q4 advance won't be out), Q2 needs to be negative. A rebounding Q2 makes a technical recession incredibly difficult to achieve inside 2025. Although Goldman Sachs downgraded general US growth due to tariffs, they still project it to be strongly positive at +1.7% for the year, largely removing the threat of consecutive true contractions. Thus, I estimate the true probability significantly lower than the crowd, although the chaos around DOGE and tariffs warrants maintaining some risk premium above the historical base rate.

Pope Francis (88) has been hospitalized since February 14, 2025, for double pneumonia, an exceptionally severe illness for an 88-year-old with a partial lung. He has required non-invasive mechanical ventilation at night and high-flow oxygen. As of mid-March, doctors report his prognosis is no longer "guarded" and he is showing slight, gradual improvement (weaning off night ventilation, concelebrating Mass in his suite, releasing a photo). The market was around 74% in early March when his condition was still guarded; with recent improvements, the immediate risk of death has somewhat decreased. Still, the 9-month mortality for an 88-year-old following a 30+ day hospitalization for severe respiratory failure is very high (easily 40-50%). Additionally, he has unsealed a pre-written resignation letter in case of long-term medical impairment, adding perhaps 15-20% probability that he steps down if he survives but remains too frail to govern. Together, these push the total probability into the 60-70% range. I estimate a 64% chance he ceases to be Pope before Jan 1, 2026.

The NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee defines a recession as a significant decline in economic activity that is spread across the economy and lasts more than a few months. They base this primarily on real GDP, real personal income, employment, industrial production, and wholesale-retail sales. Data confirms that the US economy consistently expanded through 2023 and 2024. Real GDP grew 2.9% in 2023 and 2.8% in 2024, with no negative quarters. The fourth quarter of 2024 saw real GDP increase at a healthy 2.3% annualized rate. Additionally, employment grew robustly through the end of 2024, with November and December adding 227,000 and 256,000 jobs, respectively. Because all major indicators point to steady growth through the end of the year, there is essentially zero chance that NBER will retroactively declare that a recession began in 2023 or 2024. The probability is set at the floor (2%) to account for the smallest possibility of absolutely unprecedented retroactive data revisions or rule changes.

The current legal world record is 2:00:35, set by the late Kelvin Kiptum. His tragic death in 2024 removed the most obvious candidate for a sub-two-hour marathon. The primary catalyst for recent market optimism (estimated at 0.47 in late February/early March 2025) is Jacob Kiplimo's astonishing half-marathon world record of 56:42 run on February 16, 2025. While this physiological milestone theoretically maps to a sub-two-hour marathon capability, Kiplimo is making his marathon debut at London in April 2025. Transitioning from half marathon to full marathon is notoriously unpredictable (many HM world record holders struggle at the full distance), and accomplishing a major milestone like sub-2 on debut in a legal standard race is exceptionally difficult. If his debut is promising but short of sub-2, he may make another attempt in the autumn (Berlin/Chicago, where pacing and courses are uniquely optimized for records). Because of the hurdles related to weather, legal race pacemaking, fueling, and the physical demands of extending unprecedented HM speed over 26.2 miles, the probability remains lower than a coinflip for 2025 specifically, despite Kiplimo's immense potential. I'm placing the probability at 36%, slightly lower than the market, accounting for the unreliability of debut marathons and tight timeline before end of 2025.

On March 6, the market priced Bayern Munich's title chances at 94%. At that point, Bayern held an 11-point lead over Bayer Leverkusen. Since then, Bayern has experienced a dip in domestic form, losing to VfL Bochum (3-2) on March 8 and drawing against Union Berlin (1-1) on March 15. This has allowed Leverkusen to close the gap to just 6 points at the conclusion of Matchday 26. 

However, with only 8 matches remaining in the typical 34-game season, a 6-point lead remains a towering advantage. Leverkusen would need to be near-perfect and hope Bayern loses or draws at least three more matches down the stretch. Given Bayern's historical dominance, deeper squad, and general consistency across a season, mathematical models for a 6-point lead with 8 games remaining heavily favor the leader. Given the recent drop in form and narrowing of the gap from 11 points to 6, I am adjusting the market's previous 94% estimate downward, but Bayern remains heavily favored at ~86%.

The evidence overwhelmingly points to Dune: Messiah being Denis Villeneuve's next film. Warner Bros. and Legendary have officially penciled in December 18, 2026, for an "Untitled Event Film" directed by Villeneuve, and reporting uniformly identifies this project as the third Dune film. While Villeneuve initially expressed a desire to take a break from the franchise and signed on to adapt 'Nuclear War: A Scenario', recent reports from early 2025 indicate a change of heart. Villeneuve explicitly stated he is prioritizing the script for Dune: Messiah and aims to begin filming as early as Summer/Fall 2025. Furthermore, Deadline specifically reported that 'Nuclear War' is being planned for production *after* he finishes Dune: Messiah. Given the high financial stakes and immense success of Dune: Part Two, the studio has clearly fast-tracked the third installment. The chances of another project sneaking ahead of Dune: Messiah on the release calendar are exceedingly slim, barring any unexpected health issues, massive schedule changes, or secret low-budget projects. Given the momentum and public confirmations in early 2025, I assign a 94% probability to Dune being the subject of his next film.

The inflation rate (CPI) for February 2025 printed at 2.8% year-over-year, following a hot January print (+0.5% MoM). While February's +0.2% MoM was slightly softer than the expected +0.3%, returning the YoY rate to below 2.5% by the end of 2025 faces strong headwinds. During mid-2024, inflation printed at 0.0% and -0.1%, meaning base effects in mid-2025 will mechanically push the YoY rate higher. Furthermore, impending tariffs under the Trump administration are widely expected by economists to be inflationary, putting upward pressure on consumer prices. The Manifold predictor market valued this at roughly 23% in early March before the February print. Taking into account the modest cooling in the latest data but the broader upward trajectory dictated by base effects and trade policy, a sub-2.5% reading on CPI remains unlikely but possible in a strong disinflation scenario. I estimate the probability at 24%.

The question asks whether at least three of Trump's cabinet nominees (including 15 department heads and 9 cabinet-level officials, totaling 24 initial positions) will be withdrawn or voted down by April 1, 2025. According to reports up to March 11, 2025, Matt Gaetz is the only nominee for one of these positions (Attorney General) who has formally withdrawn. All tracking sources up to March 11 confirm that out of the 24 positions, the vast majority have already been successfully confirmed by the Senate. A March 11, 2025 article explicitly notes that Elise Stefanik's nomination for Ambassador to the UN is the ONLY Cabinet-level position left to be finalized. Since only one nominee (Gaetz) has failed so far, and arguably only one position is left (Stefanik), it is mathematically impossible to reach three failures by the deadline, even if Stefanik fails (which would make two failures). Another individual, Dave Weldon, was withdrawn for CDC Director, but the CDC Director is not a cabinet-level position specified in the resolution criteria. The Manifold market explicitly prices this bet at 1% for this reason. I will output 0.02 (the system floor) because reaching three failures is essentially impossible under the rules since 23 out of 24 original positions are locked in.

The prospect of a 30-day ceasefire has advanced significantly since early March, with Ukraine officially accepting the US-led proposal. On March 11-12, Polymarket odds for Trump ending the war in his first 90 days spiked to 37%. Trump and Putin have had productive talks, and are expected to speak again the week of March 16. However, Putin has introduced caveats, questioning enforcement and Ukraine's ability to resupply, and is likely trying to extract further concessions (such as getting Ukrainian troops to surrender in Kursk) before signing. Since March 14, Russia's cautious but resistant posture suggests a drag on the momentum. I estimate a 33% chance the deal is fully agreed upon and announced by both sides before the April 19 deadline, taking into account the recent positive movement but also Putin's typical negotiating tactics that may delay or derail the timeline.

There are currently no major highly anticipated or critically acclaimed games confirmed to release in 2025 that feature live, runtime-generated AI dialogue. High-profile tools like Ubisoft's Ghostwriter and upcoming Xbox/Inworld AI implementations are either being used offline (to assist human writers during development) or are still in early prototyping stages and won't see deployment in a AAA or award-winning indie title by 2025.

For this to resolve YES, a game must feature live generative AI dialogue AND either score 90+ on Metacritic or win an award at a show prominent enough to be listed on Wikipedia's "2025 in video games" page (e.g., DICE, BAFTAs, The Game Awards). While there are small indie games using live LLM chat (like "Suck Up!"), they do not receive the critical reception necessary to meet these rigid "acclaimed" criteria (90+ score or major award wins). Furthermore, major publishers are inherently averse to deploying live LLMs due to hallucination risks, unbranded dialogue, and server costs, preventing a surprise inclusion in anticipated 2025 blockbusters. Given the 4-6 year development cycles for AAA games, the timeline is simply too tight. Thus, the probability is extremely low.

The market priced this at 0.26 on March 6. Although structural friction has emerged – most notably a heated early March Oval Office clash where Trump had to mediate between Musk and Secretary of State Marco Rubio – Trump has so far avoided publicly disparaging Musk. In fact, on March 11, Trump went out of his way to aggressively support Musk on Truth Social, explicitly announcing he would buy a Tesla to counter left-wing boycotts and defend Musk. This indicates that while internal tensions are rising (as often happens with Trump's associates), Trump is currently committed to maintaining a strong, unified public front with Musk. Because he actively defended Musk immediately after a major internal clash leaked, the near-term probability of public disparagement is slightly reduced, pulling my estimate down to 0.24.

The original release date for Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse was March 2024, but it was indefinitely delayed in July 2023 due to the Hollywood strikes. A recent article published in March 2025, citing the industry trade publication Deadline, explicitly reported that Sony has 'no plans' to release the film in 2025. Given that it is already mid-March 2025 and there is no release date, marketing campaign, or finished production, it is impossible for such a massive animated blockbuster to be released before the end of the year. The Manifold crowd correctly priced this at a 1% probability in early March 2025. Consequently, the probability of release in 2025 is as close to zero as possible.

Tom Brady retired from playing in the NFL following the 2022 season (in early 2023). On October 15, 2024, NFL team owners unanimously approved his bid to purchase a 5% minority stake in the Las Vegas Raiders. According to the NFL's Collective Bargaining Agreement (CBA) and league rules, active players are strictly prohibited from holding ownership stakes in an NFL franchise. To bypass this, it would require a nearly impossible vote of approval from other team owners or for him to completely divest his newly acquired slice of ownership. In addition to his ownership duties, Brady is now firmly established in his highly lucrative 10-year, $375 million broadcasting role as the lead color commentator for Fox, which he has confirmed he will return to for the 2025 season. At age 47 (turning 48 in August 2025), a return to the field is both physically extraordinary and contractually/legally prohibited by his current ownership status. The market traded at 4% earlier and there is zero recent credible traction for him playing again. The April 2024 comments in the prompt happened before his ownership was officially approved in October 2024. Therefore, I assign a 2% probability.

The probability of Boris Johnson returning as the leader of the Conservative Party by January 1, 2026, is effectively zero. Kemi Badenoch recently became leader (November 2024) and despite some grumblings, party insiders are giving her until the May 2026 local elections to prove herself. Removing a newly elected leader within less than a year would require a massive and unprecedented internal revolt, especially so soon after the historic 2024 general election defeat where stability is valued. 

Furthermore, Boris Johnson is currently not a Member of Parliament, having resigned in June 2023. For him to become leader, he would first need to find a safe seat, have the incumbent MP step down, win the resulting by-election, and then win a leadership contest within the next 9 months. Johnson himself stated in April 2024 that it is "unlikely" he will stand as an MP again "in the short term." There is no credible movement or mechanism currently in motion to facilitate his return on this highly constrained timeline. Thus, I estimate this at the lowest possible probability boundary.

Multiple independent laboratories and scientists have analyzed the data related to Avi Loeb's 'spherules' from the IM1 crash site and have concluded that they are likely entirely terrestrial in origin, specifically matching the chemical composition of coal ash or industrial pollutants. Given that the resolution criteria require a peer-reviewed systematic review incorporating 3 independent labs to determine with 95% confidence that the material is both of non-terrestrial and technological origin by the end of 2025, the likelihood of this happening is as close to zero as possible. The scientific consensus heavily weighs against Loeb's hypothesis, and any systematic review submitted to reputable peer review would face significant opposition. Further, no such systematic review with these findings has even been drafted or submitted as of early 2025.

The community estimate is 0.01. Extensive recent evidence from early 2025 (e.g., Sam Altman's blog posts in January and February) confirms that OpenAI currently views itself as having a path to AGI, and describes upcoming models as pointing toward AGI ("systems that start to point to AGI are coming into view"), indicating they do not view GPT-5 itself as constituting AGI. Furthermore, OpenAI has mapped out a 5-level trajectory to AGI with Level 5 being organizational capability. Given these definitions and recent leadership statements emphasizing incremental breakthroughs (like o1 and o3) rather than an abrupt singular arrival of full AGI with GPT-5, the chance of an on-the-record exact claim from leadership or an official post explicitly stating "GPT-5 is AGI" within 30 days of release is extremely minimal. Therefore, I predict 2%.

OpenAI has firmly committed to restructuring into a for-profit Public Benefit Corporation in 2025. A major legal hurdle was cleared on March 4/5, 2025, when a federal judge denied Elon Musk's request for a preliminary injunction, explicitly stating he was unlikely to succeed on the merits. This paves the way for OpenAI to proceed without an immediate court-ordered freeze. 

The primary risk to a 2025 completion is the ongoing investigation by the California Attorney General, who is scrutinizing the transfer of charitable assets. Because OpenAI's operations and IP are valued in the hundreds of billions, regulatory review and valuation disagreements could drag out. OpenAI has until October 2026 for its investors, so if the AG process is complex, they could theoretically afford a slight slip into 2026. 

However, OpenAI is highly motivated to complete this within 2025 as planned. Since it is a Delaware nonprofit, it may have structural avenues to negotiate a settlement (such as assigning equity to a distinct charitable foundation) and rapidly transfer control of its commercial operations by year-end. The resolution criteria only requires that commercial operations cease to be *governed* by the nonprofit board, which could be effectuated even if valuation disputes over the nonprofit's equity stake are still ongoing. The community prediction was 0.89; I am slightly more conservative at 0.83 due to the lingering risk of regulatory delays by the CA AG pushing the finalization past Jan 1, 2026.

The EUR/USD pair requires a drop below 1.0000 to resolve YES. Since 2024, it has approached this level, bottoming at 1.0350 in late December 2024 and 1.0257 in early January 2025. Although numerous late-2024 macro forecasts from major banks predicted parity by late 2025 due to potential U.S. tariffs and an aggressive ECB easing cycle relative to the Fed, recent March 2025 data shows a bullish reversal for the Euro. Weakening U.S. data has prompted expectations of near-term Federal Reserve rate cuts, pushing the pair back towards the $1.08-$1.10 range. The requirement is merely to *touch* <1.0000 at any point before 2026, which historically has roughly double the probability of *closing* below a barrier, but the recent upswing means an ~8-10% decline is now needed within ~9.5 months. Given the strong psychological support at parity and the recent reversal reducing momentum, I am adjusting downwards slightly from the early March community prediction of 35% to 28%.

The forecast resolves as YES if it is not reported by NYT, WSJ, or a major international agency that Iran possesses a nuclear weapon by July 31, 2025. As of early March 2025, U.S. intelligence and the IAEA consistently assess that Supreme Leader Khamenei has not yet made the political decision to weaponize. Even if that decision were made today, experts and intelligence estimate it would take 'a matter of months' (often cited as around six months) to construct even a crude nuclear weapon. Given that there are only 4.5 months remaining until the July 31 deadline, the physical timeline is exceptionally tight. Furthermore, any clandestine progress would not immediately equate to 'possession' reported by the NYT, WSJ, or IAEA unless publicly tested or definitively revealed. Lastly, Israeli strikes in October 2024 destroyed the Taleghan 2 facility, disrupting potential nuclear trigger research, setting back the timeline further. The probability of Iran possessing a nuclear weapon before the expiration of the timeline in July 2025 is exceedingly small.

The record for a 3x3x3 under 3.44 seconds is a historically exceedingly rare feat, achieved by only 3 people ever (Max Park, Ruihang Xu, and Yiheng Wang). As of March 16, 2025, no fourth person has broken this threshold (Luke Garrett remains closest with an exact 3.44s from 2023). Although Garrett and others are actively competing and setting records in other events (e.g., Garrett's OH WR in March 2025), there is no evidence that anyone besides the excluded individuals has achieved a sub-3.44s solve. Given that there are only 14 days remaining until the March 31 deadline, the likelihood of a brand new competitor emerging to achieve this historic time during this specific short window is extremely low. Therefore, the probability is set to the minimum allowed bound (5%).

The resolution of this question depends almost entirely on whether the Liberal Party of Canada (LPC) wins more seats than the Conservative Party of Canada (CPC). Given the CPC's high floor of safe regional seats in Western Canada, it is virtually impossible for them to finish third, making first or second place the only realistic outcomes. 

Historically (2019 and 2021), the CPC has finished second in seats despite winning the popular vote due to the LPC's structural vote efficiency in Ontario and Quebec. The race has recently reverted to this exact dynamic. Following Justin Trudeau's resignation, Mark Carney was elected Liberal leader on March 9, 2025 (search_0_summary). This caused a massive leadership 'honeymoon' bounce for the Liberals. An Abacus Data poll published March 16, 2025, shows the CPC's popular vote lead has shrunk to just 4 points (search_0_result_0). Consequently, the most recent 338Canada projection from March 16 places the two parties in a dead heat: LPC at 150 seats, CPC at 149 seats (search_2_result_2).

While the current projection is roughly a 50/50 toss-up, the Liberal surge is likely at its absolute peak immediately following their leadership convention. The honeymoon effect is expected to fade slightly going into an election (which must occur by October 2025, and may be called as early as April/May). The CPC still holds the structural advantage in combating general incumbent fatigue (overall cost of living and housing issues). The market priced this at 0.33 on March 6 (before Carney's official win). Incorporating the full polling bounce, I rate the probability of the CPC coming in second at 41%.

The likelihood of regular, active-duty US military personnel physically entering Gaza before July 2025 is extremely low. Early February 2025 comments by President Trump regarding possible US deployment for rebuilding Gaza briefly raised speculation, but he walked those comments back a day later. Currently, the US is implementing its role in the January 2025 ceasefire by utilizing private US security contractors at checkpoints (alongside Egyptian contractors), as Israeli forces withdraw. Crucially, the resolution criteria strictly exclude contractors, Special Operation Forces, advisors, and high-ranking diplomats. This eliminates the most plausible edge cases for early deployment. The historical US aversion to placing "boots on the ground" in Gaza—even during complex logistical operations like the 2024 temporary pier—remains in effect. With the June 2025 deadline fast approaching and peacekeeping/reconstruction currently delegated to regional partners, the UN, and contractors, it is highly unlikely that regular conventional US troops will be deployed to Gaza in the next 3.5 months.

The forecast date is March 16, 2025, leaving only 4.5 months until the July 31, 2025 deadline. A WHO pandemic declaration requires sustained, widespread human-to-human transmission. As of early March 2025, both the WHO and CDC explicitly state that no sustained human-to-human transmission of H5N1 has been identified and the risk to the general public remains low. Even if a human-adapted variant were to emerge immediately, the timeline for it to spread globally and prompt a formal pandemic declaration by the WHO is extremely tight (COVID-19 took ~3 months). Prediction aggregators placed the risk at roughly 5% at the start of the year, and with less time remaining and no material change in transmission dynamics, the actual probability is objectively lower now. The previous market price of 9% likely reflects a premium placed on tail risks and reactions to individual cases (e.g., the first US death in Jan 2025), but the underlying epidemiology does not support a high chance of a pandemic by July 2025. There is a strong consensus among intelligence agencies and military analysts that China's target for having the capability to invade Taiwan is 2027, not 2025. An assessment by Janes in late December 2024 assigns a 75% probability to ongoing coercion strategies without military action in the next 6-12 months, and only a 5% chance of a "seaborne invasion/military operation." Recent geopolitical analyses (e.g., Recorded Future in Feb 2025, think tank consensus) uniformly assess a near-term invasion as unlikely, particularly with the unpredictability surrounding the new Trump administration's actions and no immediate signals of the massive military buildup that would be required for such an operation. While unexpected escalations like the seizure of an inhabited outlying island (Kinmen, Matsu, Pratas) represent a tail risk that satisfies the resolution criteria, the probability remains very low. Setting my estimate at 5%, matching the quantified assessment from Janes and reflecting the absence of imminent warning indicators.

The Polymarket market price for Luxembourg to win Eurovision 2025 was 0.00 as of early March 2025. This extremely low probability is corroborated by betting odds, which placed Luxembourg near the very bottom (around 35th out of 37) of the likely winners in early 2025 following their national selection. Without any recent spikes in popularity—recent betting summaries focus entirely on countries like Sweden, Austria, France, and Italy—it is statistically near-impossible for Luxembourg to suddenly secure a win. The base probability of winning as a random guess without any prior information would be ~2.7% (1 in 37), but given their consistent bottom-tier ranking by bookmakers, the true probability is heavily penalized to effectively zero. Therefore, I confidently estimate a 2% chance (the lowest allowed probability in this system).

NOAA's National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI) explicitly calculated a 4% probability that 2025 will be the hottest year on record as of their February 2025 assessment. The UK Met Office similarly forecasts that 2025 will be the third-warmest year, trailing 2024 and 2023. The transition to a La Niña climate pattern is currently underway and typically leads to global cooling relative to El Niño years (such as 2024). While January 2025 was exceptionally warm for global land, February 2025 ranked only 3rd warmest globally. Given the strong consensus from major climate agencies and the known cooling impact of La Niña, the chance of 2025 setting a new record is very low, aligning perfectly with the NOAA statistical forecast of 4%.

The Denver Nuggets are currently 43-24 and stand in 2nd/3rd place in the Western Conference as of mid-March 2025. They hold a 4.5 to 5 game lead over the play-in spots (7th and 8th seed) with approximately 15 games remaining. Even in the unlikely scenario where they slip out of the top 6, they would enter the Play-In tournament where they would remain heavy favorites to secure a playoff spot. Star player Nikola Jokic is listed as "probable" with a minor elbow contusion and ankle impingement but continues to play, recently posting historic numbers. The Polymarket price was 0.99 over a week ago, and their position remains intact. Thus, they are extremely likely to make the playoffs.

Lithuania is currently not a favorite to win Eurovision 2025. Following their selection of the rock song "Tavo akys", their betting odds initially placed them around 15th place in late February. Recent odds from March show Sweden, Austria, and France dominating the top spots. Lithuania has historically never won the contest, and their current standing in bookmaker predictions reflects a very low probability of an overall victory. The Polymarket trading price of 1% further matches the implied probability derived from their betting odds given they remain a mid-tier contender. Therefore, 2% represents a conservative but confident upper bound on their chances.

The most recent known value for VEE on the Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' is 'No'. There are currently no widely approved human vaccines for Venezuelan equine encephalitis (only the IND TC-83 and C-84 vaccines, and veterinary vaccines), and obscure Wikipedia lists have a very low base rate of being updated unless a major breakthrough occurs and receives widespread media coverage. Given the slow nature of pharmaceutical development (especially for rare diseases) and Wikipedia's latency in reflecting such updates, it is highly unlikely that a vaccine will be fully developed, licensed, and updated on this specific Wikipedia list by the resolution dates within the next year. Thus, the probability remains close to the lower limit of 0.05, slowly relaxing towards 0.10 for the 1-year horizon to account for unforeseen developments or minor editorial changes.

The forecast dates span from March 2025 to March 2026. Goryachkina's FIDE ranking on 2025-03-16 is #5 with a rating of 2548. She enjoys a solid 20-point cushion over the #6 player (Koneru Humpy, 2528). By contrast, she is only 13 points away from the #2 player (Ju Wenjun, 2561). This creates a distinct "top 5" tier, making her much more likely to retain or improve her rank than to fall out of it. 
For 2025-03-23, no new FIDE list will be published, meaning her rank will definitively remain #5 (p=0.98 to account only for unpredictable admin or wiki edge-cases). 
For April 2025, her Nicosia WGP results will be factored in, but she is currently playing well and holds a strong advantage; falling 20+ points in one tournament while #6 ascends is quite improbable (p=0.94).
For the 3-6 month window, typical rating volatility introduces some chance she falls below #5, though her 5-year track record as a top 5 regular keeps confidence high (p=0.88 -> 0.82).
For the 1-year horizon (March 2026), she has an approximately 74% chance of remaining in the top 5, grounded in base rates for elite players retaining status over a rolling 1-year window combined with her age and active prime-form trajectory.

Kateryna Lagno is currently exactly Rank 7 on the initial March 2025 FIDE list, tied in rating with #8 Anna Muzychuk (2516) and only 2 points ahead of #9 Zhu Jiner (2514). Due to this cluster of closely-rated competitors directly beneath her, she is highly vulnerable to dropping in rank from even one minor rating loss or a competitor's gain. 
For the first date (March 23, 2025), the active list will still be the March list, securing a near-guaranteed YES. For April and beyond, the inclusion of the late-February Monaco Grand Prix (where she finished tied for 4th) and future events make her position precarious. Given age factors (she is 35) relative to rising stars like Zhu Jiner, and the asymmetric risk (more close competitors below than above), her probability of maintaining or improving upon Rank 7 steadily decreases over longer horizons.

Diphyllobothriasis is a parasitic infection caused by a fish tapeworm. It is easily and safely treatable with anthelminthic medications like praziquantel. Because of the availability of effective, cheap treatment and the relatively low incidence of the disease (especially in terms of morbidity/mortality), there is absolutely no active pursuit of a human or veterinary vaccine for this parasite. A check of the current Wikipedia article "List of infectious diseases" confirms that the "Vaccine(s)" column for Diphyllobothriasis is blank, indicating 'No'. The likelihood of a vaccine being developed, tested, and publicized enough to be added to this Wikipedia page between now and March 2026 is practically zero. The only risk for a 'Yes' resolution would be temporary Wikipedia vandalism or a misunderstanding by an editor (e.g., confusing a treatment with a vaccine), but such errors are typically reverted quickly. Following the guidelines to slightly increase uncertainty over time, I assess the probability as extremely low across all time horizons, rising only marginally for the 2026 date.

Venezuelan hemorrhagic fever is a rare, neglected tropical disease caused by the Guanarito virus. There are currently no approved vaccines. A search of recent scientific literature reveals that vaccine candidates are entirely in pre-clinical stages, such as DNA vaccine testing in animal models (guinea pigs) dating back to 2016, with no imminent human trials or late-stage candidates close to approval. Vaccine development protocols naturally require years for phase trials, safety reviews, and regulatory approval. It is extraordinarily unlikely that a completely novel vaccine for this rare pathogen will be developed, approved, and updated on the specific Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' table by any of the forecast dates up to March 2026. Given the extremely long baseline timeline for vaccine development, probabilities are kept at the absolute 0.02 floor for dates in 2025, with a fractional increase to 0.03 for 2026 to account for minor tail risks such as Wikipedia vandalism that isn't reverted in time.

Vaccine development protocols naturally require years for phase trials, safety reviews, and regulatory approval. It is extraordinarily unlikely that a completely novel vaccine for this rare pathogen will be developed, approved, and updated on the specific Wikipedia 'List of infectious diseases' table by any of the forecast dates up to March 2026. Given the extremely long baseline timeline for vaccine development, probabilities are kept at the absolute 0.02 floor for dates in 2025, with a fractional increase to 0.03 for 2026 to account for minor tail risks such as Wikipedia vandalism that isn't reverted in time.

There is currently no licensed vaccine for Group A streptococcal infection. Clinical trials are only in early stages (Phase 1), with one such trial estimated to complete in late March 2025. Typically, a vaccine requires successful Phase 2 and Phase 3 trials, regulatory review, and approval, which takes several years. Consequently, it is virtually impossible for a Strep A vaccine to be fully developed, licensed, and listed on the specified Wikipedia page by March 2026. The only pathway for a "YES" resolution is anomalous Wikipedia editing (e.g., vandalism) that goes uncorrected on a resolution date, which is highly unlikely but anchors the probabilities slightly above 0.

There is currently no approved vaccine for Toxoplasmosis in humans, and no candidates are in late-stage clinical trials that could realistically be approved by March 2026. While a veterinary vaccine (Toxovax) exists for sheep, the Wikipedia "List of infectious diseases" page focuses on humans and currently lists "No" for the Toxoplasmosis vaccine status. For the table to list "Yes" on any of the resolution dates, it would require either an unprecedented rapid approval of a human vaccine, or an erroneous Wikipedia edit (e.g., vandalism or a misapplied inclusion of the animal vaccine) that survives precisely on the resolution date. Such uncorrected modifications on highly visible tables in Wikipedia are relatively rare and usually reverted quickly. Consequently, the probability remains very low, with a slight increase over longer horizons to account for the marginally higher chance of unexpected events or prolonged Wikipedia edit disputes over time.

The FIDE rating listed on Wikipedia for Nakamura on March 16, 2025, is 2802. A 1% increase means he would need an official FIDE rating of at least 2830.02. In classical chess history, only four players have ever officially crossed 2830 on a FIDE list (Carlsen, Kasparov, Caruana, Aronian). Nakamura's peak FIDE rating is 2816. At the 2800 level, gaining 28 rating points requires an extraordinarily dominant tournament performance (e.g., scoring +6 or more unbeaten against top-tier Super GMs), which is extremely implausible in modern top-level chess, particularly given Nakamura's relatively lower frequency of classical chess events compared to his prime, as well as his focus on streaming and online rapid/blitz formats. Additionally, Wikipedia updates the top 100 FIDE rankings only once a month matching FIDE's official monthly release. Thus, the near-term dates (March 23, April 15) have functionally 0% chance of reflecting such a massive rating spike, as there are neither the events nor the mathematical opportunity for such an increase. Even extending the horizon to March 2026, achieving a 2830+ rating is exceedingly unlikely. I am setting the probabilities effectively to the minimum permitted limit.

The most advanced Lyme disease vaccine candidate is VLA15 (Pfizer/Valneva), which completed its primary Phase 3 vaccination series in July 2024. A booster dose trial phase will occur around July 2025, and Pfizer has officially stated they plan to seek FDA approval in 2026. Because the regulatory submission won't even happen until 2026, the probability of having an approved and available vaccine by any of the resolution dates (the latest being March 16, 2026) is virtually zero. Additionally, while a Lyme disease vaccine previously existed (LYMErix) but was discontinued in 2002, the Wikipedia article currently leaves the vaccine column blank (resolving as "No"). This implies historical/discontinued vaccines are not counted. The only plausible paths to a "Yes" resolution are a surprise early regulatory submission in another market or a Wikipedia editor prematurely adding a candidate/discontinued vaccine to the list that is mistakenly parsed as a "Yes". This error risk increases slightly with the forecast horizon.

Black piedra is a minor superficial fungal infection of the hair, treated simply with antifungal shampoo or shaving. There are currently no approved fungal vaccines for humans at all, and a trivial condition like black piedra has zero economic or medical incentive to warrant vaccine development. The Wikipedia page 'List of infectious diseases' currently lists no vaccine. The only conceivable way this could resolve 'Yes' is if the Wikipedia page is vandalized or erroneously edited on the exact resolution date to list a vaccine. Because this page is monitored and the disease is obscure, the chance of this happening and persisting precisely on those dates is astronomically low. Consequently, I assign probabilities at the lowest allowable floor, increasing only very slightly over time to account for the infinitesimally greater opportunity for Wikipedia anomalies over longer horizons.

To resolve YES, Lei Tingjie's Elo must reach at least 2577.52 (effectively 2578) from her current 2552. Given her K-factor of 10, a +26 point gain requires outperforming her expected score by roughly +2.6 across her next rating periods—which demands a dominant classical streak, like scoring 8/10 against equal 2550-rated opposition. 

- March 23, 2025: The Wikipedia FIDE page typically updates monthly. By Mar 23, it will still show the March 1 list (2552). Even if it updates earlier, Lei is not playing in March (having withdrawn from the March 14-25 Cyprus WGP). Probability = 0.02 (the absolute minimum allowable based on structural impossibility).
- April 15, 2025: The April FIDE list will be out. Because she played no FIDE-rated classical games in March, her rating on this list will carry over as exactly 2552. Probability = 0.02.
- June 14, 2025: This will reflect the June 1 FIDE list. She participates in Norway Chess Women starting May 26 and ending around June 6. Because the tournament concludes in June, it will NOT be rated on the June 1 list, meaning her June rating will rely only on any obscure local events in April/May. A +26 shift by June 1 is enormously unlikely. Probability = 0.03.
- September 12, 2025: Reflects the September 1 list, which will incorporate Norway Chess and any summer tournaments. A +26 gain would require an exceptional performance in Norway Chess (e.g., scoring +6 against elite women) or dominating multiple opens. Top women’s ratings are stable, making this a tall order, but slightly more plausible given the games played. Probability = 0.07. 
- March 16, 2026: A one-year horizon allows more variance. Reaching a career peak of 2578 is difficult for an established 28-year-old top player who currently sits at world #4, but it is plausible if she goes on a tremendous year-long run. Probability = 0.14.

To resolve YES, Lei Tingjie's Elo must reach at least 2577.52 (effectively 2578) from her current 2552. Given her K-factor of 10, a +26 point gain requires outperforming her expected score by roughly +2.6 across her next rating periods—which demands a dominant classical streak, like scoring 8/10 against equal 2550-rated opposition.

A +26 shift by June 1 is enormously unlikely. Probability = 0.03.

Anand is rank 14 on March 16, 2025, with a rating of 2743. 'Higher ranking' means a rank of 14 or better (i.e., Rank ≤ 14). FIDE updates rankings monthly on the 1st. For the March 23 date, the rankings will not have updated, so the March 1 Wikipedia ranking of 14 will still be active (p=0.96). For future dates, the probability decreases. Anand plays classical chess very rarely, mostly just in the Bundesliga or European Club Cup, and just dropped from 10th to 14th after losing rating points in February. He is closely trailed by highly active players (Dominguez at 2741, Fedoseev and Duda at 2739). Given his age (55), declining activity, and the very tight pack of active players just below him, it is significantly more likely that he drifts downward (to 15th or lower) than upward. The probability of him maintaining a rank of 14 or better declines steadily over the forecasting horizon, dropping below 50% by the summer of 2025.

The resolution depends on whether a vaccine is added to the "Bolivian hemorrhagic fever" row in the "Vaccine(s)" column of the Wikipedia page "List of infectious diseases". Currently, this field is blank. There is no FDA-approved vaccine for Bolivian hemorrhagic fever (caused by the Machupo virus); research primarily involves animal trials and early-stage candidates. Even the Junin virus (Argentine hemorrhagic fever), which has a localized vaccine (Candid #1), lacks a vaccine entry on this same Wikipedia page, demonstrating that this list is not frequently updated with localized or experimental countermeasures. Given the absence of late-stage clinical trials, there will likely be no major news to prompt a Wikipedia editor to update this specific page in the next year. Thus, the probability of the article indicating "Yes" is extremely low and relies largely on the faint possibility of an arbitrary or erroneous edit. Uncertainty slightly increases for dates further out.

Gukesh's current official FIDE rating (and Wiki listed rating) for March 2025 is 2787. A 1% increase requires him to reach ~2815 (or ~2822 if the benchmark is 2794). At this stratosphere of chess, gaining 28 points is exceptionally difficult since drawing against 2750-rated players loses points, meaning he would need multiple historic 2900+ performances. Furthermore, the FIDE list only officially updates at the start of each month, meaning for March 23 the rating will almost certainly be unchanged from March 16. For April 15, he is not playing classical chess in March, so the April list will likely also remain stagnant. By June 14, he may only have concluded Norway Chess (which may not even make the June 1 list), giving almost zero time to accumulate 28 points. By September and next March, he will play more events, increasing the long-shot chance that he goes on an unprecedented hot streak to hit 2815+.

Evgeniia Chikunova currently holds the 200m breaststroke long course world record with a time of 2:17.55, set in April 2023. This time shattered the previous record by 1.4 seconds, an exceptionally large margin for this event. Her nearest active competitors are over 1.5 seconds behind her best time, making it highly unlikely that someone else will surpass her purely on athletic merit within the next year. 

The main risks to her losing the record are a sudden, rapid improvement by a competitor (e.g., at the 2025 World Aquatics Championships in July/August), or the record being stripped due to a retroactive doping suspension (a non-trivial risk given her Russian federation affiliation, though no current allegations have surfaced). If Chikunova breaks her own record, she will still hold the world record and the question would resolve YES. Because of the enormous 1.4-second buffer she has over the field, probabilities remain very high but slowly decay over time as more major meets (like the World Championships and national Olympic/World trials) inject baseline uncertainty. I follow standard forecasting guidelines capping the absolute ceiling at 0.95 for near-term dates and tapering down toward 0.84 for a year out.

The March 16 Wikipedia page relies on the FIDE March 1 list, placing Harika Dronavalli at rank 16. Since FIDE's rankings update on the 1st of every month, the March 23 date will overwhelmingly likely point to the exactly identical March 1 list, yielding a ranking of 16 (thus 'as high or higher' is True by equality). Starting from mid-April, new ratings from events like the Nicosia Women's Grand Prix will reshape the list. Harika is ranked 16th (2483), extremely close to players above (Kosteniuk, 2484; Vaishali, 2484) and below (Assaubayeva, 2476; Divya, 2470). Therefore, her ongoing ranking position is extremely volatile and highly dependent on short-term results, representing a near 50-50 chance of staying in the top 16 vs dropping below it. As the horizon expands from mid-2025 to 2026, the probability structurally converges to maximum uncertainty (0.50), reflecting the unpredictable long-term variance in individual player form, possible rating swings by young rising stars trying to overtake her, and standard regression to the mean within a tightly packed Elo bracket.

Since FIDE's rankings update on the 1st of every month, the March 23 date will overwhelmingly likely point to the exactly identical March 1 list, yielding a ranking of 16 (thus 'as high or higher' is True by equality). Starting from mid-April, new ratings from events like the Nicosia Women's Grand Prix will reshape the list. Harika is ranked 16th (2483), extremely close to players above (Kosteniuk, 2484; Vaishali, 2484) and below (Assaubayeva, 2476; Divya, 2470). Therefore, her ongoing ranking position is extremely volatile and highly dependent on short-term results, representing a near 50-50 chance of staying in the top 16 vs dropping below it. As the horizon expands from mid-2025 to 2026, the probability structurally converges to maximum uncertainty (0.50), reflecting the unpredictable long-term variance in individual player form, possible rating swings by young rising stars trying to overtake her, and standard regression to the mean within a tightly packed Elo bracket.

The resolution criteria asks if Anish Giri will have a ranking "as high or higher" than on 2025-03-16. His current rank on March 1, 2025 is #19. For March 23, the monthly update will not have happened yet, so Wikipedia will almost certainly still show him at #19. For April and beyond, his ranking will reflect the March 'Prague Chess Festival' where he performed decently but lost to Aravindh Chithambaram, who had a phenomenal 2830 TPR tournament and jumped to #14 in the world live rankings. Aravindh jumping ahead pushes everyone below #14 down by one spot. Meanwhile, Le Quang Liem (formerly #18) performed poorly and likely drops below Giri. This creates a tight race where Giri could either be barely #19 or drop to #20/#21. Because he lost absolute Elo points and was overtaken by a rising Aravindh, it is more likely than not that he falls to #20 or lower on the April list. Over longer horizons, Giri's age (slightly past his peak compared to new rising Indian stars) naturally pushes his probability of maintaining a top 19 spot slowly downwards, though his high floor keeps him competitive.

The question tests whether Wikipedia will list 'Yes' for a vaccine for 'Viral pneumonia' on specific dates. Scientifically, viral pneumonia is a syndromic condition caused by multiple distinct viruses (e.g., influenza, RSV, SARS-CoV-2), rather than a single pathogen. While vaccines exist for some specific viruses that cause viral pneumonia, a universal 'viral pneumonia vaccine' does not exist and is conceptually inaccurate. As such, encyclopedic standards strongly favor keeping the vaccine status as 'No' (or leaving it blank, mapped to 'No'). The slight probability of resolving 'Yes' comes primarily from the chance of subjective Wikipedia edits (e.g., an editor changing it to 'Yes, for some causes' which might be counted as 'Yes') or forecaster manipulation that inadvertently survives until a resolution snapshot. Given Wikipedia's strong moderation and the specific nature of disease classification, the outcome is highly likely to remain 'No'. I assign minimum probabilities that slowly increase over the forecast horizon to account for unforeseen changes in Wikipedia consensus or edge-case edit snapshots.

The broader 60-day trend for ACGL has been slightly negative (dropping from ~107 in late 2024 to the low 90s), and the statistical trend analysis provided by the yfinance tool estimates a 40-47% chance of the stock increasing past 92.57 in the short term due to this short-term downward drift and negative seasonal precedence. Given the instruction that weekly moves are largely unpredictable and we should stay close to 0.5 without a very strong signal, I adopt a moderately bearish short-term prior of 0.45 to account for potential mean-reversion following the recent two-day spike against a prevailing downtrend. 

Following the explicit instruction that "uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5", I regress my estimates monotonically towards 0.50 for the longer lock-up periods. Although standard asset pricing would generally suggest long-term probabilities of exceeding current prices rise due to positive positive market drift, the specific constraint to pull distant estimates "closer to 0.5" mandates this structurally reverting forecast.

The recent stock action shows clear downward short-term momentum, falling from $48.88 on March 10 to $45.57 on March 14. This is supported by negative fundamental news, primarily weak 2025 guidance ($4-$4.75 adjusted EPS) due to soft agricultural market margins and biofuel policy uncertainty detailed in their February earnings report. Combining the short-term negative trend with the statistical linear model (P = 0.48 for > 45.57) gives a slightly bearish short-term outlook. However, as the forecast horizon expands, high uncertainty, typical long-term positive equity drift, ongoing stock buybacks (100M extended program), and relative undervaluation (P/E ~10x) pull the probabilities back towards and eventually slightly above the 0.5 baseline, reflecting mean reversion from current multi-year lows.

The closing price on 2025-03-14 (Friday before the forecast date) was 80.73. The stock experienced a major ~20% drop following its February 2025 earnings report, trading between ~76 and ~87 in the weeks since. Currently at 80.73, the stock is historically undervalued relative to even the most conservative analyst price targets (such as Craig-Hallum's $90), indicating slight positive pressure for mean reversion in the short term. However, the company faces fundamental headwinds ('disruption well into 2026' due to TikTok transitioning away and a sales restructuring), meaning any recovery will be sluggish and uneven. Following the explicit prompt instructions that weekly stock moves stay close to 0.5 and that uncertainty should strictly push probabilities closer to 0.5 for distant forecasts, I assign slightly bullish short-term estimates that gradually decay toward a coin flip over the longer time horizons.

AMD's market price on the Friday before the valuation date (2025-03-14) closed at $100.97. According to the fetch_ts_yfinance statistical tool, and utilizing short-term momentum and historical seasonal strength, the probability that the price exceeds $101 on 2025-03-23 is estimated at 0.57. I am setting this 0.57 tool inference as the primary anchor for the initial resolution date. The stock has been highly volatile, descending from earlier historic highs down to roughly $100 but shows recent upward momentum (3 of the last 4 days). Because short-to-medium-term stock movements inherently behave more like a random walk over a long duration, any predictive edge diminishes the further into the future we predict. ANSS is in the process of being acquired by SNPS for $197 in cash and 0.345 shares of SNPS per ANSS share. As of mid-March, SNPS recently traded near $446.23, implying a deal value of roughly $350.95. The most recent ANSS prices were trading at a slight discount to this deal value (around $330-$340) due to pending regulatory approvals, most notably China's SAMR. Recently (March 3), Synopsys issued $10B in bonds to fund the purchase, and both the UK and Japan have cleared the transaction, signaling that the deal closing is highly imminent. 

Because ANSS trades at a discount to the implied deal value, the stock has a strong structural tailwind (positive expected drift) as the deal approaches closing and the spread narrows. Even if SNPS's price declines moderately (up to ~3-8%), the realization of the deal spread acts as a buffer, making it more likely than not that ANSS's price on future dates will end up strictly higher than its March 16 baseline. As horizons extend, there's greater risk that a larger market correction drags SNPS down enough to wipe out the spread buffer. Thus, probabilities logically start favorably above 0.5 and regress toward 0.5 over time according to instructions.

The forecast date is March 16, 2025 (a Sunday), so the target threshold defaults to the closing price from Friday, March 14, which was exactly 126.11. The stock has shown strong local momentum, rising from ~122 to 126.11 over the preceding week. Because of this short-term upward momentum, the short-term probability (1 week out) of being higher than 126.11 is around 54%. However, on April 1, 2025, CAH has its ex-dividend date, which will mechanically reduce the share price by its $0.5056 dividend amount. This will slightly pull down the probability of remaining strictly above the current threshold for the April 15 resolution date. Moving to the longer-term resolution dates (June 2025 through March 2026), the initial momentum signal is entirely diluted by standard market volatility. While equities generally have a positive drift over a 1-year horizon, the regular quarterly dividend payments (removing ~$2.02 effectively over the year) negate roughly 1.6% of this growth when considering unadjusted share price. Abiding by the rules to increase uncertainty (push toward 0.5) over longer horizons, the probabilities smoothly curve down from 0.54 at 1 week out to 0.50 at the 1 year mark.

The short-term trend for CHRW is negative over the past three months, with the stock falling from a peak of ~107 in January to around 96.55. The yfinance trend analysis indicates relatively low probability (040-0.47) of exceeding the threshold in the next week, so the near-term probability starts below 0.5. However, Wall Street analysts maintain a consensus price target around $117 to $119, suggesting significant long-term upside potential, and Q1 earnings are projected to represent strong YoY growth. Therefore, as the time horizon extends to 1 year, the probability of closing above 96.55 gradually reverts to and slightly crosses 0.5, driven by historical long-term market drift and favorable growth projections.

1. **Reference Class & Base Rate**: For an individual mid-to-large cap stock, the probability of a positive return relative to today's price over short horizons (1 week to 6 months) is close to 50%. While general market drift offers a small positive equity premium, high idiosyncratic volatility creates a 'volatility drag' that pulls the median return down, meaning the probability of increasing is typically only a coin flip (~49-52%).
2. **Recent Momentum & Market Evidence**: The required `fetch_ts_yfinance` tool failed to load data, but web searches established that DAY severely gapped down in early March. The stock price as of March 6 had fallen to $57.23, significantly below its 50-day moving average of $69.27. Analyst and news sentiment have been turbulent, with recent articles (e.g., Forbes on March 5) criticizing the stock as overvalued and earnings releases missing expectations ($0.01 vs $0.42 expected). 
3. **Forecast by Horizon**: Due to the severe recent drop, short-term momentum is negative. In the very near term, highly volatile and newly downtrending stocks generally have a slightly lower chance of immediate recovery due to post-earnings drift and sustained selling pressure. Thus, for the 1-week and 1-month horizons, I assign a probability slightly below 50%. 
4. **Uncertainty Scaling**: As the forecast horizon extends to 3 and 6 months, the effect of the March negative catalyst will decay. Muted slightly by the broader long-term equity risk premium, the expected probability naturally reverts towards maximum uncertainty (0.50), strictly adhering to the prompt instruction to have probabilities for distant dates move closer to 0.5.

2. **Recent Momentum & Market Evidence**: The required `fetch_ts_yfinance` tool failed to load data, but web searches established that DAY severely gapped down in early March. The stock price as of March 6 had fallen to $57.23, significantly below its 50-day moving average of $69.27. Analyst and news sentiment have been turbulent, with recent articles (e.g., Forbes on March 5) criticizing the stock as overvalued and earnings releases missing expectations ($0.01 vs $0.42 expected). 
3. **Forecast by Horizon**: Due to the severe recent drop, short-term momentum is negative. In the very near term, highly volatile and newly downtrending stocks generally have a slightly lower chance of immediate recovery due to post-earnings drift and sustained selling pressure. Thus, for the 1-week and 1-month horizons, I assign a probability slightly below 50%. 
4. **Uncertainty Scaling**: As the forecast horizon extends to 3 and 6 months, the effect of the March negative catalyst will decay. Muted slightly by the broader long-term equity risk premium, the expected probability naturally reverts towards maximum uncertainty (0.50), strictly adhering to the prompt instruction to have probabilities for distant dates move closer to 0.5.

The threshold price for March 16 (resolving to March 14 due to the weekend) is 97.56. DIS has experienced a steep drop in the last two weeks, falling from around 113 to 97.56 (a >16% decline). However, there is no major fundamental bad news for the company reported; its Q1 2025 earnings beat expectations (EPS up 35%), and the CEO provided strong forward guidance for the rest of 2025. This points to a likely mean reversion where the stock has a higher probability of rebounding in the near term. The fetched time series tool estimates a combined probability of 0.61 for the first date based on trend constraints and seasonality. Following the instructions to shrink probabilities toward 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends, I am setting the near-term probability to 0.60 and gradually decaying my confidence toward 0.53 for the longest-term date over a year out, despite the upward long-term drift typical of stocks.

The task requires estimating if the DLTR stock price will be higher than the 2025-03-14 close of $64.56 across five future dates. I anchor my estimate strongly on the `fetch_ts_yfinance` tool's explicitly provided P(increase) of 0.61. However, given recent downward general momentum (the stock has declined from ~$130 over the past year) and short-term volatility following the Q4 earnings report on March 12, I'm moderating this anchor towards 0.5, as weekly stock predictions are highly uncertain. Additionally, following the explicit instruction that uncertainty should INCREASE with longer horizons, the probabilities smoothly regress towards 0.5 for the later dates.

The base market forecast for stock prices over time is a random walk, with the probability of exceeding the current price regressing strongly toward 0.5 in the long run. The baseline price matches the close on March 14, 2025 ($33.68). Notably, March 14, 2025, was exactly the record date (and thus ex-dividend date under T+1 rules) for their $0.24 quarterly dividend, meaning the benchmark price is clear of this immediate liability.

In the short run, Devon Energy shows a moderately positive linear trend (+0.03/day over the last 60 days) and upward momentum, justifying a slight >0.5 edge (55%) for the 1-week and 1-month horizons. Moving into mid-2025, the U.S. EIA projects tight oil markets with Brent around $74 and WTI around $70 before transitioning into oversupply later in the year.

As the forecast horizon extends to late 2025 and early 2026, two headwinds appear: (1) the EIA forecasts U.S. supply to push WTI crude prices lower (closer to $62 into 2026), and (2) DVN will undergo several more ex-dividend dates ($0.24+ each quarter), providing structural price drag. Thus, the probabilities regress steadily toward 0.5 as uncertainty grows, eventually dipping slightly below 0.50 (49%) for the 1-year horizon in 2026 due to the fundamental headwinds and dividend mechanics.

The stock just had a massive 1-day rally on the forecast date (March 14), pushing it up ~4% to $446.23, which forms a local peak relative to the last 2 weeks (hovering around 430). This automatically makes it harder to achieve a 'higher' price in the very short term due to mean reversion. Additionally, EG will go ex-dividend for a $2.00 quarterly payment on Monday, March 17 (the exact start of the forecast period), which introduces an instant mechanical headwind of ~$2 to the share price. Furthermore, the stock has been suffering from a slew of recent analyst downgrades (some with price targets in the 340-389 range) due to a $1.7 billion Q4 reserve charge that caused a large loss. Despite these headwinds, the company is generating strong operational cash flows and has authorized a massive 10 million share buyback, which will provide downside support and long-term tailwinds. Balancing the short-term negative mean reversion, the $2 ex-dividend reduction, and analyst downgrades against historical stock market drift and strong buybacks, I assign lower probabilities for the near-term dates and gradually revert towards a long-term base rate of 55%.

The stock recently dropped from ~93 at the beginning of March to 86.82 on March 14, 2025. This drop roughly coincides with the ex-dividend date on Friday, March 14, 2025 (amounting to $0.83 drop on its own, plus perhaps broader market or cyclicality adjustments). The forecast starts from an arguably 'depressed' ex-dividend spot, historically increasing the chances of rebound over subsequent horizons. The fetch_ts_yfinance gives P(value > 86.82) = 0.55 using the linear model and 0.61 overall when combining seasonality. Since the March 14 price has the dividend already factored out (ex-dividend date was March 14 per search_1), the current price provides a clean base. Looking ahead: in Q1/Q2/Q3 2025 the stock will accrue additional earnings and eventually go ex-dividend again in June/September, but generally equity drift is positive. For the first date (1 week out, March 23), momentum might remain noisy but drift is slightly positive. The drop already accounts for the dividend, removing that headwind. I assign slightly above 0.5 probabilities stepping up mildly over time due to equity risk premium. No significant news exists to assume catastrophic drop or massive surge.

ERIE has seen significant volatility in March 2025, dropping from ~440 to ~412. This drop coincides with a broader market correction happening around March 12-14 (S&P 500 closed in correction territory on March 13). The YFinance tool gives a short-term trend probability of 0.41 for an increase by March 23. This suggests short-term momentum is negative. However, over the medium-to-long term, stocks generally revert linearly towards their drift which is usually positive. Also, Erie Indemnity's recent Q4 profit was strong (profits rose 37%) and EPS growth is expected to continue for 2025. I am factoring in negative short-term momentum for the nearest date, setting it to 0.5. As the forecast horizon extends, I apply a modest positive drift factor in line with general stock market returns, especially for a profitable financial company, yielding slightly higher probabilities: 0.51 for April, 0.52 for June, 0.53 for September, and 0.55 for March 2026. Weekly stock moves (especially trying to guess an exact date comparison) are largely unpredictable, so sticking close to 50% makes sense, but the longer the horizon, the higher the generic probability of a stock exceeding its starting point due to underlying positive drift.

For the subsequent resolution dates extending to 2026, the generalized upward drift of the equity market, particularly for a solid consumer defensive stock with a ~3.8% dividend yield, gently reasserts itself. Probabilities trend smoothly upwards from the near-term momentum-driven 0.43, crossing 0.5 around the 3-month mark, and reaching 0.56 for the 1-year horizon. This embodies increasing uncertainty returning towards base-rates, while accounting for the market's long-term positive expectancy slightly above 0.5 for a 1-year frame.

Kellanova (K) is currently pending an all-cash acquisition by Mars, Inc. at $83.50 per share, expected to close in H1 2025. With the latest known close dates trading firmly in the $82.25-$82.60 range (below the acquisition price), the stock functions similarly to a fixed-income instrument yielding to a maturity date. Therefore, it is expected to slowly drift upward toward the $83.50 mark under the time value of money as the close date approaches. If the deal closes successfully before a distant resolution date, the stock will delist and its final price of $83.50 will be used, forcing a definitive YES resolution. Although this underlying economic dynamic would normally suggest that confidence (and thus certainty of a YES) increases over longer horizons as the deal approaches finalization, I am strictly adhering to the prompt's explicit constraints: "Your uncertainty should INCREASE with forecast horizon — probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5" and "Weekly stock moves are largely unpredictable — stay close to 0.5." To comply, I have anchored the first week's probability at 0.58 to reflect the upward merger drift and then linearly regressed the probabilities for future dates closer to 0.5.

The threshold value is roughly 220.01 (the close on March 14, 2025, which acts as the March 16th price since the 16th is a Sunday). Lowe's experienced a sharp drop from $237 to $220 between March 10 and March 14. Web search confirms this was not due to poor company-specific news (Lowe's had relatively solid recent earnings guidance and an updated $253 valuation by Forbes in early March), but rather a severe overall market selloff driven by macro policy uncertainties (Trump tariffs and recession fears sent the S&P 500 into a correction). The stock is now significantly oversold relative to its 2024–2025 range.

Due to the oversold condition, a short-term bounce is favored. The statistical time series model generated a linear estimate of P(>220) = 0.55 and a seasonal historically matched estimate of 0.67, with a combined probability of 0.61. I have tempered this estimate down slightly to 0.57 for the first resolution date to respect the unpredictable nature of weekly stock moves and the strong downward macro momentum over the last 5 days. 

In strict adherence to the instruction that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon and probabilities for distant dates should be closer to 0.5, I decay the initial 0.57 probability monotonically toward 0.51 for the 1-year horizon.

The underlying question is whether LUV's stock price will be higher than its closing price on Mar 16, 2025. Given it currently sits around $31.10 after an 8% surge on Mar 11 due to the new baggage policy, it's starting from a local high. Historically, such spikes are prone to short-term mean reversion (as the yfinance linear model projects a drift down to $28.4). Consequently, the probability of exceeding the $31.10 target explicitly on Mar 23 is below 50% (set to 46%). 

As the horizon moves outward, base rate stock drift begins pulling probabilities upward. While the structural revenue increase expected from baggage fees (est. up to $1.5B) supports longer-term momentum and most analysts' median targets appear centered around $32-34 (which implies an upside to $31.10), their overall recommendation distribution sits heavily at "Neutral/Hold" rather than strong buys. Southwest's earnings report hits on April 24, which will add volatility and could expose the actual revenue-minus-lost-market-shares offset. For these reasons, the probabilities for mid-to-long term horizons align closely with historical base rates of stock market upside (51-56%).

The historical trend analysis for MCK yields a short-term probability of 0.41 that the price will be higher next week, as the stock has exhibited some mean reversion after closing at $647.9. Since weekly stock movements are largely random walks, the probabilities should be close to 0.5, anchoring moderately on the statistical model for the near term while reverting toward 0.5 over longer horizons as explicit predictive power decays. I am forecasting probabilities of 0.44 for next week and smoothly escalating towards 0.50 for the 1-year horizon, balancing normal long-term upward drift against the rule that uncertainty increases with time (pushing probabilities closer to 0.50). MCK recently raised full-year guidance, suggesting solid fundamentals, but the overarching factor over the given horizons is systemic volatility and noise, making a forecast right around the midline appropriate.
